---
title: "Charissa Fay Corazon Borja-Tabora, MD, FPPS, FPIDSP"
---

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
![Head - Infection Prevention and Control Unit (IPCU)](img/charissa_tabora.png)

-   Dr. Tabora is a Medical Specialist III and Head of the Infection Prevention and Control Unit (IPCU) at the Research Institute for Tropical Medicine (RITM). She is a Fellow and Diplomate of the Philippine Pediatric Society and the Pediatric Infectious Disease Society of the Philippines. 
-   She leads infection prevention and control (IPC) programs, healthcare epidemiology initiatives, and capacity-building activities for healthcare facilities nationwide, including training programs under the Asian Pacific Society of Infection Control (APSIC). 
-   Her work focuses on strengthening infection prevention, healthcare-associated infection surveillance, antimicrobial stewardship, and outbreak preparedness, contributing to national public health policies and healthcare quality improvement. (Research Institute for Tropical Medicine)
-   She is an active speaker and contributor to national and international scientific meetings on infection prevention, healthcare epidemiology, and infectious diseases. 

:::
